behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: This is local lint-rule logic on template text. Rejected Integration because the behavior text is explicitly a static lint check, not cross-module runtime assembly or I/O-dependent behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the lint rule with positive/negative template fixtures, including verbatim and near-verbatim phase-instruction leakage patterns from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff (template + interpolation + fixtures + assembly). Rejected System because no explicit external contract/round-trip side effect is required by the behavior text. Rejected Agentic because this checks prompt construction, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real in-process harness assembly path and assert the final prompt string has no phase-specific instruction leakage patterns.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly says validate behavior from loaded skill context; correctness depends on model output changing due to that context, which mocks can hide. Rejected Workflow because this does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one live model call where the harness loads skill files at runtime; verify observed behavior aligns with skill-context-driven expectations and is not satisfied by prompt-only hard-coded rules.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness/config wiring across modules and filesystem variants. Rejected Agentic because the behavior only requires running with unchanged prompt text across skill variants, not judging model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run two in-process harness invocations (normal vs degraded/missing SKILL.md) with identical prompt text and assert only skill-file inputs changed.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model behavior degrading when skill context regresses; deterministic stubs can mask this. Rejected Workflow because this is a single behavior check under one prompt, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model A/B test with identical prompt and harness settings, varying only SKILL.md quality; assert degraded/missing variant fails structural behavior criteria.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering/pipe integrity across model-client wrapper, artifact writer, and parser modules. Rejected System because behavior text does not require external contract validation beyond local artifact creation. Rejected Agentic because content quality is irrelevant; ordering is the target.
behaviors.test_description: Use real harness flow with controlled model response, then assert diagnostic artifact stores raw output and timestamps/order prove capture occurred before parsing/extraction code path.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Core requirement is assertion logic semantics (structure-aware vs keyword-only), which can be proven with local fixtures. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require full harness/runtime interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with crafted outputs where keywords pass but structure is wrong; ensure structural validators fail those cases and pass correctly structured cases.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection based on actual model outputs; mocks risk false confidence. Rejected Workflow because it describes one model-output evaluation, not multiple model-dependent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke test and compare outcomes of structural assertions versus keyword-only checks on the same outputs; assert at least one real regression is caught only by structural checks.
